summary: HYPOTHESIS FALSIFIED. 60-day hold does NOT improve E1. Sharpe drops: 20d=0.658 → 60d=0.300 → 90d=0.251. Avg R improves (0.282→0.355→0.371) but trade count halves and win rate collapses (38.8%→26.7%→25.6%). Best pair at 60d: USDJPY (+0.478 Sharpe), worst: USDCAD (+0.125). NOTE: This standalone rate-sign-consistent backtest shows positive R at 20d (vs original state_allocator -0.186R) — difference is signal definition: 5-day consistency filter removes noise. The 5-day filter alone may be worth testing in state_allocator.

HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED AS REJECTED. March JPY seasonal: Pre-2020 (BOJ passive, n=6): 67% WR but avg R barely negative (-0.058). Post-2020 (BOJ/Fed active, n=5): 20% WR, avg R +1.948 against thesis (USD strengthened massively). 2022 worst year: USDJPY +6% in March during Fed hike panic. April shows no reversal (45% rose, +0.57% avg). Note: data only available 2014-2024, not 2010-2024. Key insight: the seasonal exists weakly pre-2020 but was never statistically robust. The 2022 Fed hike cycle permanently overwhelmed it. Not tradeable as standalone.

Pure carry (rate-sign, 3xATR stop) mostly negative Sharpe. Only GBPUSD (+0.178) and AUDUSD (+0.174) positive. USDJPY (-0.555) and EURUSD (-5.636) worst — large adverse moves vs high differentials. Current best carry: GBPJPY (447bps) and USDJPY (441bps) are actually the WORST performers due to BOJ vol. AUDNZD differential is 0bps right now — carry irrelevant. Key finding: pure carry-as-direction does not work. v004 works because it layers multiple signals. Carry income contributes but price momentum must align. Recommended pairs for carry overlay: GBPUSD and AUDUSD only.

CRITICAL features identified. Library has 63 patterns: 26 CRITICAL (severity>=2), 20 Elevated, 12 Bull, 5 Neutral. SHALE_SUPPLY_OIL_CRASH (primary match) features: momentum_decay dominant (-0.551), gold_weak (-0.429), ABOVE 200SMA (+0.427) — this is actually bullish equity price action during an oil crash. The match is commodity/macro-driven not equity-dislocation. CRITICAL fingerprint across all 26 CRITICAL patterns: consecutive_down_weeks (signal=2.22), sector_dispersion (1.99), realized_vol_90d (1.69), realized_vol_30d (1.46), realized_vol_10d (1.46). Implication: SHALE match is triggering on OIL-era macro conditions, not equity dislocation. The bull regime floor fix (0.50x) was correct. Full get_feature_weights() method recommended for AlexandrianLibrary.

TIMING GATE CONFIRMED BY BACKTEST. UTC 03xx Grade A: WR=56.2% avgR=+1.078 (n=16). UTC 04xx Grade A: WR=18.2% avgR=-0.295 — disaster. UTC 04xx Grade A+: WR=0% avgR=-1.000. The live UTC 03xx timing gate (deployed 2026-05-22) is validated by historical backtest. UTC 05xx shows high avgR (+1.357, n=14) but all GBPUSD — appears to be window overlap artifacts, not a real edge. UTC 14xx (NY): Grade A WR=31.9% avgR=-0.214 — confirms NY_PM veto is correct.
action: NONE_REQUIRED — timing gate already deployed and validated. UTC 15xx worth monitoring as London close plays emerge.

summary: COMPONENT IC ANALYSIS COMPLETE (n=208 trades). market_structure: IC=-0.195 (confirmed anti-edge, HYP-034 already deployed). fvg_tap: IC=-0.056 overall — BUT this is CONDITIONAL. Within UTC 03xx (live gate), fvg_tap=2 shows WR=50% avgR=+1.208 (n=18) vs overall fvg_tap=0 bias. The anti-edge is driven by bad timing windows (UTC 04xx, NY session) — NOT the UTC 03xx window. DO NOT zero-weight fvg_tap. displacement: IC=+0.068 — only genuinely positive component. displacement=0 trades worst (WR=27.8% avgR=-0.076). kill_zone: IC=0.000 — completely flat, no predictive value but required for setup identification. sweep: IC=+0.039 — weak positive.
new_hypothesis: HYP-NEW: fvg_tap anti-edge is timing-conditional. The 'perfect FVG tap' is only harmful in late London / NY. Within UTC 03xx, fvg_tap=2 is actually the strongest sub-group. No action needed — but informs weight tuning.
action: MONITOR displacement score as primary quality signal. displacement=0 = skip the trade. This is the one component that genuinely predicts direction.

summary: EMPIRICAL PROP CHALLENGE MC: 97.9% pass rate (n=2000 sims). Uses actual London A R-multiples (28 trades, WR=42.9%, avgR=+0.893) as empirical distribution. Config: $100k Lucid, 0.75% risk, +8% target, -8% floor. Median 11 trades to pass (p25=6, p75=17). Median peak DD=2.2%, p90=5.5%. This VALIDATES the theoretical MC estimate of 90.3-100%. The challenge should pass comfortably once live trades start flowing. G4 IS NOW GREEN — library threat cleared to 0.00 (was 1.00 HALT_NEW).
action: G4 is GREEN. Only G2 (0/30 live trades) is RED. Paper trades can now flow — ICT is in NORMAL mode.

task_name: Fixed TP/SL structure vs trailing stop — v004 8-pair sweep

headline: Displacement=0 veto: REJECTED globally, CONFIRMED for London Grade A only
summary: Global: displacement=0 removes 16.1% of ICT trades, avgR delta=+0.038 — below +0.05 threshold. REJECTED as universal veto. London Grade A specifically: disp>0 avgR=+0.869 vs disp=0 avgR=+0.250. London A+A+ combined: delta=+0.059 (14.5% of trades removed) — barely clears threshold. Recommendation: London-only soft filter, not a hard gate. NYC_PM: disp=0 actually has marginally higher WR (36% vs 31.9%), confirming NY_PM is already compromised.
action: Wire displacement>0 as +0.1 score bonus in London session scoring, not a veto.

headline: UTC 15xx as second window: REJECTED (avgR=+0.090)
summary: UTC 15xx: n=36, WR=44.4%, avgR=+0.090. High WR but near-zero avgR means no edge. The WR is deceptive — the avgR doesn't support adding this as a window. UTC 05xx (avgR=+1.200, n=20) is entirely London session GBPUSD at different clock times — not a separate window, just variance. UTC 16xx is anti-edge (WR=18.8%, avgR=-0.672). UTC 14xx anti-edge (WR=30.2%, avgR=-0.266). Confirmed: UTC 03xx is the only confirmed positive window. Current timing gate is correct.
action: No change. UTC 03xx gate stands. UTC 05xx is London session variance, not a separate window.
data_source: data/forensics/trade_forensics.jsonl (n=310 ICT trades, UTC hour breakdown)

headline: USDCAD retirement forensics: trailing stop exit is the specific failure mechanism
summary: USDCAD forensics confirm retirement was correct. Trailing stop exits: WR=17.6% avgR=-0.493 (n=34) — devastating. Time exits: WR=60.8% avgR=+0.400 (n=74) — actually solid. 50% of USDCAD losses are COMMITMENT_FAILUREs (61/122 trades) — highest ratio of all pairs. Interpretation: USDCAD produces macro-aligned signals that fail to follow through. The trailing stop designed to lock in gains instead gets triggered by noise before the move develops. GBPJPY failure profile is different: more THESIS_FAILURE and REGIME_FAILURE — its signals were structurally wrong, not timing-wrong. Both retirements validated, but for different reasons.
action: No change. If USDCAD is ever reconsidered: time-based exits only, no trailing stops.

headline: Equity momentum gates: flat results, no clean regime gate found in signal dataset
summary: Analyzed 1,007 equity signals from signals_20260409_222335.csv. Key results: VIX level shows no clean gate (VIX<15: 37.9% WR, VIX>30: 37.6% WR — essentially flat). 200 SMA above/below: 38.3% vs 38.4% — no differentiation. Strong momentum filter (RSI>60 + ADX>25 LONG): 40.6% WR vs 36.8% baseline — slight edge, pnl near-zero. 1d continuation vs mean-reversion: 39% vs 35.9% — minor edge for continuation. Interpretation: this signal dataset lacks a strong momentum-based gate. The equity system's edge likely comes from the game_score + macro gates, not raw momentum. The ATR calibration fix (May 18) is more important than adding momentum filters. RQ-AUTO-004 closed — no new gates recommended.
action: No change to equity system. Focus momentum research on Sharpe-decomposed per-ticker analysis if needed later.

title: Displacement Gate >= 1.5 Confirmed: +0.146R/trade, 81% kept
finding: London trades: disp>=1.5 gate removes 33 low-quality trades (WR=15%, avgR=-0.167) and improves remaining 139 trades from baseline avgR=+0.448 to +0.594. Net gain: +0.146R per kept trade, 81% of trades pass gate. Recommend wiring disp>=1.5 as soft veto in pipeline Stage 3. Colin confirmation required before live wire — touches pipeline logic.
action: Add HYP-045 to ledger. Flag for Colin to confirm pipeline wiring.

title: SURPRISE: ICT Score Inverted — score 7-8 beats score 9-11 within high-disp trades
finding: Within high-displacement (disp>=1.5) London trades, ICT score is INVERSELY correlated with performance. score[7-8): WR=52%, avgR=+1.210 (BEST). score[8-9): WR=36%, avgR=+0.790. score[9-10): WR=15%, avgR=-0.293 (WORST). score[10-11): WR=0%, avgR=-1.000. Grade A (score_avg=8.0): WR=45%, avgR=+0.982 vs Grade A+ (score_avg=9.3): WR=20%, avgR=+0.019. The A+ downgrade to A decision was directionally correct but may understate the problem. Hypothesis: ultra-high-score setups are too obvious — professionals have already positioned. The score scale may need to be inverted or capped at A-level for entry decisions.
action: HYP-045: Wire score cap / inversion study. Do NOT wire live without backtest.

title: HYP-035 ATR Compression: Forensics data insufficient — need raw OHLC for ATR percentile
finding: Forensics data contains post_entry_atr_ratio (price move / ATR), not pre-entry ATR percentile rank. These measure different things. Proxy analysis: trades entering with atr_ratio [1.0-1.5) are worst (WR=46%, avgR=+0.092, n=370). Trades with low post_entry atr_ratio [0-0.5) are best but n=17 only. Cannot properly test HYP-035 (entry during ATR compression) without raw OHLC + 252-day ATR percentile. Task needs network access to fetch historical data. Defer to live session.
action: RQ-022 remains QUEUED but needs network — not suitable for REST MODE.

title: System Health: NORMAL — threat 0.29, no stops, both systems clear
finding: Cross-system bridge: ict_mode=NORMAL, quant_signal=NORMAL. library_threat_score=0.29 (was 1.00 CRITICAL on 2026-05-24, now cleared). library_primary_regime: VALUE_RATE_REGIME_SHIFT. commitment_score_avg=0.47 (borderline — watch). ict_stops_24h=0, ict_commitment_failures_24h=0. State file updated 2026-05-25 23:48 UTC. All clear.

title: COMBO FILTER: score<=8.5 + disp>=1.5 → WR=47%, avgR=+1.105 (baseline: 31%, +0.448)
finding: Score cap sweep + displacement gate combination tested on 172 London ICT trades. Best practical combo: score<=8.5 AND disp>=1.5 keeps 50% of trades (n=86) at WR=47%, avgR=+1.105. Baseline: WR=31%, avgR=+0.448. Delta: +16ppt WR, +0.657R per trade. Tighter (score<=8.0 + disp>=1.5): WR=52%, avgR=+1.210 but only 26% of trades. This is the most impactful ICT filter discovered since UTC 03xx gate. Needs MC re-validation before live wiring.
action: Run MC sim on score<=8.5+disp>=1.5 config. Wire only after MC confirms >70% pass rate.

title: Score cap sweep complete — score<=8.5 confirmed optimal threshold
finding: Full sweep across [7.5, 8.0, 8.5, 9.0, 9.5, 10.0, 11.0] on London ICT. Baseline n=172, WR=30.8%, avgR=+0.448. score<7.5: WR=20% (worse than baseline — too restrictive). score<8.0: n=62 (36%), WR=43.5%, avgR=+0.923. score<8.5: n=110 (64%), WR=38.2%, avgR=+0.745. score<9.0: n=131 (76%), WR=36.6%, avgR=+0.718. On UTC03 base: score<8.5 gives WR=60.7%, avgR=+1.295 (n=28). score<8.0 gives WR=60% but n=15 (too small, 0.8/month). score<=8.5 is the optimal threshold: maximum avgR at meaningful trade count.
action: Wire score<=8.5 cap in pipeline. Pending HYP-048 UTC03 gate decision.

title: Triple filter: WR=72.7%, avgR=+1.670 — but 27 months to G2
finding: All three gates stacked (UTC03 + disp>=1.5 + score<=8.5): n=22 over 20 months, WR=72.7%, avgR=+1.670, MC pass rate=100%, median 6 trades to pass prop challenge. BUT trade frequency: 1.1/month → 27 months to collect G2 (30 live trades). This is theoretically the best ICT filter ever found. Operationally impossible for prop challenge data collection.
action: Do not wire as primary filter. May be valid for high-conviction sizing after G2 is collected.

title: STRATEGIC FINDING: UTC03 gate is the prop challenge bottleneck
finding: Rate-quality analysis reveals UTC03 gate is the binding constraint on G2 timeline. UTC03 base: 1.8/month → G2 in 17 months. Adding score<=8.5 on UTC03: 1.4/month → G2 in 21 months. Dropping UTC03, using disp>=1.5+score<=8.5 instead: 4.3/month → G2 in 7 months, MC=99.8%. The disp>=1.5+score<=8.5 filter achieves nearly identical quality to UTC03 (MC 99.8% vs 99.7%) at 2.4x higher trade frequency. Prop challenge recommendation: switch primary quality filter from UTC03 to disp>=1.5+score<=8.5. UTC03 restriction can be re-applied post-challenge for live trading if desired.
action: REQUIRES COLIN DECISION. See RQ-REST-006 and HYP-048.

title: HYP-036 exit ladder: MFE_3bar insufficient proxy — needs actual trade log
finding: Attempted exit ladder optimization using forensics MFE_3bar field. Problem: MFE_3bar captures max excursion within first 3 bars only, not overall trade trajectory. Exit ladder simulation using this proxy produced negative avg R for all configs — clear sign that MFE_3bar ≠ exit R in a TP ladder structure. Winner MFE_3bar distribution: min=0.10, median=2.27, mean=3.04. 68.6% of all London trades show MFE_3bar >= 0.5R within first 3 bars. This is directionally useful (momentum is real early) but cannot drive exit optimization. Need: actual trade log with exit price, time_to_exit, TP/SL reason for each trade.
action: HYP-036 moved to NEEDS_DATA. Add exit logging to paper_trading.py if not present.
